sentences.text: The current Privileged specification defines mhpmevent CSRs to select and control event counting by the associated hpmcounter CSRs, but provides no standardization of any fields within these CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0

sentences.text: For at least Linux-class rich-OS systems it is desirable to standardize certain basic features that are broadly desired (and have come up over the past year plus on RISC-V lists, as well as have been the subject of past proposals).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0

sentences.text: This enables there to be standard upstream software support that eliminates the need for implementations to provide their own custom software support.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0

sentences.text: This extension serves to accomplish exactly this within the existing mhpmevent CSRs (and correspondingly avoids the unnecessary creation of whole new sets of CSRs - past just one new CSR).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0

sentences.text: This extension sticks to addressing two basic well-understood needs that have been requested by various people.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0

sentences.text: To make it easy to understand the deltas from the current Priv 1.11/1.12 specs, this is written as the actual exact changes to be made to existing paragraphs of Priv spec text (or additional paragraphs within the existing text).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The extension name is "Sscofpmf" ('Ss' for Privileged arch and Supervisor-level extensions, and 'cofpmf' for Count OverFlow and Privilege Mode Filtering).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Note that the new count overflow interrupt will be treated as a standard local interrupt that is assigned to bit 13 in the mip/mie/sip/sie registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The following bits are added to `mhpmevent`:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: [cols="^1,^1,^1,^1,^1,^1,^1,^1",stripes=even,options="header"] | |63 |62 |61 |60 |59 |58 |57 |56 |OF |MINH |SINH |UINH |VSINH |VUINH |WPRI |WPRI |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: [cols="15%,85%",options="header"] | | Field | Description | OF | Overflow status and interrupt disable bit that is set when counter overflows | MINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in M-mode is inhibited | SINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in S/HS-mode is inhibited | UINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in U-mode is inhibited | VSINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in VS-mode is inhibited | VUINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in VU-mode is inhibited | WPRI | Reserved | WPRI | Reserved |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: For each ``x``INH bit, if the associated privilege mode is not implemented, the bit is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: Each of the five ``x``INH bits, when set, inhibit counting of events while in privilege mode ``x``.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: All-zeroes for these bits results in counting of events in all modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: The OF bit is set when the corresponding hpmcounter overflows, and remains set until written by software.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: Since hpmcounter values are unsigned values, overflow is defined as unsigned overflow of the implemented counter bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: Note that there is no loss of information after an overflow since the counter wraps around and keeps counting while the sticky OF bit remains set.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: If supervisor mode is implemented, the 32-bit scountovf register contains read-only shadow copies of the OF bits in all 29 mhpmevent registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: If an hpmcounter overflows while the associated OF bit is zero, then a "count overflow interrupt request" is generated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: If the OF bit is one, then no interrupt request is generated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: Consequently the OF bit also functions as a count overflow interrupt disable for the associated hpmcounter.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: Count overflow never results from writes to the mhpmcountern or mhpmeventn registers, only from hardware increments of counter registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: This count-overflow-interrupt-request signal is treated as a standard local interrupt that corresponds to bit 13 in the `mip`/`mie`/`sip`/`sie` registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: The `mip`/`sip` LCOFIP and `mie`/`sie` LCOFIE bits are, respectively, the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for this interrupt. ('LCOFI' represents 'Local Count Overflow Interrupt'.)
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: Generation of a count-overflow-interrupt request by an `hpmcounter` sets the associated OF bit.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: When an OF bit is set, it eventually, but not necessarily immediately, sets the LCOFIP bit in the `mip`/`sip` registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: The LCOFIP bit is cleared by software before servicing the count overflow interrupt resulting from one or more count overflows.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: The `mideleg` register controls the delegation of this interrupt to S-mode versus M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control

sentences.text: This extension adds the `scountovf` CSR, a 32-bit read-only register that contains shadow copies of the OF bits in the 29 mhpmevent CSRs (mhpmevent3 - mhpmevent31) - where scountovf bit X corresponds to mhpmeventX.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control > Supervisor Count Overflow (`scountovf`) Register

sentences.text: This register enables supervisor-level overflow interrupt handler software to quickly and easily determine which counter(s) have overflowed (without needing to make an execution environment call or series of calls ultimately up to M-mode).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control > Supervisor Count Overflow (`scountovf`) Register

sentences.text: Read access to bit X is subject to the same mcounteren (or mcounteren and hcounteren) CSRs that mediate access to the hpmcounter CSRs by S-mode (or VS-mode).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control > Supervisor Count Overflow (`scountovf`) Register

sentences.text: In M-mode, scountovf bit X is always readable.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control > Supervisor Count Overflow (`scountovf`) Register

sentences.text: In S/HS-mode, scountovf bit X is readable when mcounteren bit X is set, and otherwise reads as zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control > Supervisor Count Overflow (`scountovf`) Register

sentences.text: Similarly, in VS mode, scountovf bit X is readable when mcounteren bit X and hcounteren bit X are both set, and otherwise reads as zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Sscofpmf" Extension for Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering, Version 1.0 > Count Overflow Control > Supervisor Count Overflow (`scountovf`) Register
